etymology.description: phonetic 晶(晶) (jīng, 'twinkling stars') + phonetic 生(生) (shēng) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted twinkling stars with 晶(晶) (jīng, 'twinkling stars') indicating the twinkling or shining quality. The phonetic component 生(生) (shēng) was added to support the pronunciation but ultimately did not carry a separate semantic load. This compounded form solidified the modern representation of 'star'.

examples.chinese: 夜空中有许多闪烁的星星(夜空中有許多閃爍的星星)。
examples.pinyin: Yèkōng zhōng yǒu xǔduō shǎnshuò de xīngxing.
examples.english: There are many twinkling stars in the night sky.

examples.chinese: 她的眼睛像星星一样明亮(她的眼睛像星星一樣明亮)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yǎnjing xiàng xīngxing yīyàng míngliàng.
examples.english: Her eyes are as bright as stars.

examples.chinese: 孩子们在数星星(孩子們在數星星)。
